comments.text:
Hi Sean, could you take a look at this intermittent failure?

comments.author: sean.skim21@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
I think I had this issue few times on Try when I worked on this patch (I believe that it had to do with the timing of [`MutationObserver`](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/a9824b3ffc2efea7a317f114bc3192ccdffa0ef7/netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js#29) when removing each expected messages), but it passed when I reran the test. 

Also it seems like more than [90% of the failures](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/intermittent-failures/bugdetails?startday=2024-09-19&endday=2025-01-17&tree=trunk&failurehash=all&bug=1928537) comes from Linux. I am wondering if it would be a good idea to disable the test on Linux.
comments.creator: sean.skim21@gmail.com

comments.author: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.raw_text:
Bug 1986649 changed the test to also check the statuspanellabel for the
expected string, however that value is not always synchronized with
the onStatusChange event, leading to intermittent failures.

This change fixes it so that we allow both "Looking up ..." and
"Transferring data from ..." status strings.
It is still possible that the test would be racy (other status panel
values are possible), but all current failures have one of these two strings.
comments.creator: valentin.gosu@gmail.com
comments.text:
Created attachment 9536305
Bug 1928537 - Fix raciness in browser_necko_l10n.js r=#necko


Bug 1986649 changed the test to also check the statuspanellabel for the
expected string, however that value is not always synchronized with
the onStatusChange event, leading to intermittent failures.

This change fixes it so that we allow both "Looking up ..." and
"Transferring data from ..." status strings.
It is still possible that the test would be racy (other status panel
values are possible), but all current failures have one of these two strings.

comments.raw_text:
I think I had this issue few times on Try when I worked on this patch (I believe that it had to do with the timing of [`MutationObserver`](https://searchfox.org/mozilla-central/rev/a9824b3ffc2efea7a317f114bc3192ccdffa0ef7/netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js#29) when removing each expected messages), but it passed when I reran the test. 

Also it seems like more than [90% of the failures](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/intermittent-failures/bugdetails?startday=2024-09-19&endday=2025-01-17&tree=trunk&failurehash=all&bug=1928537) comes from Linux. I am wondering if it would be a good idea to disable the test on Linux.

comments.text:
Created attachment 9536305
Bug 1928537 - Fix raciness in browser_necko_l10n.js r=#necko


Bug 1986649 changed the test to also check the statuspanellabel for the
expected string, however that value is not always synchronized with
the onStatusChange event, leading to intermittent failures.

This change fixes it so that we allow both "Looking up ..." and
"Transferring data from ..." status strings.
It is still possible that the test would be racy (other status panel
values are possible), but all current failures have one of these two strings.

bug_analysis.root_cause: The test netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js intermittently times out during execution, likely due to resource constraints or timing issues related to networking and localization components within the browser's networking stack.
symptoms: Console warning about Quirks Mode in test page
OOM (Out of Memory) related crash signatures reported in linked crashes
reproduction_steps: Run netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js test in automated CI environment
Observe intermittent timeouts during test_domain_change subtest
Check logs for test timeout and memory bloat/leak warnings
bug_analysis.technical_details: The bug is an intermittent test failure in the networking mochitest browser_necko_l10n.js, showing test timeouts and memory bloat logs via XPCOM_MEM_BLOAT_LOG. Crash reports linked show OOM crashes in JS GC allocation paths. Failures occur primarily on linux1804-64 platforms with opt and debug builds, but also on Windows and macOS. Failures are sporadic with low failure rate (~0.003 to 0.011 failures per push).
bug_analysis.user_impact: No direct user impact as this is a test failure in the CI system; however, it may mask regressions in networking localization or cause delays in automated testing and integration.

refined_analysis.root_cause: The mochitest 'netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js' intermittently times out on the 'test_domain_change' test due to suspected race conditions and memory bloat during event dispatch in Necko's networking stack, specifically in dom/network/Connection.cpp and Connection.h.
symptoms: Console warnings related to Quirks Mode during test execution
OOM crashes with JS GC allocation and XPCOM_MEM_BLOAT_LOG memory bloat entries
affected_components: Event dispatch and resource management in dom/network/Connection.cpp and Connection.h
reproduction_steps: Run 'browser_necko_l10n.js' mochitest on linux1804-64 opt/debug CI builds
Observe intermittent timeouts during the 'test_domain_change' subtest
Check CI logs for OOM crashes and memory bloat logs (XPCOM_MEM_BLOAT_LOG)
refined_analysis.technical_details: Failures occur sporadically (~0.3%-1.1% per push) on Linux, Windows, and macOS. Crash reports implicate race conditions in event dispatch involving mozilla::dom::Event::WidgetEventPtr and JS GC allocation paths. No existing fixes address this issue.
refined_analysis.user_impact: No direct user impact; test flakiness slows CI and risks masking regressions in networking localization.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: Investigate event dispatch functions in dom/network/Connection.cpp/h used by 'test_domain_change'. Add detailed logging around event dispatch and memory usage. Profile JS GC allocations during test runs. Introduce synchronization or delays around event dispatch to reduce race conditions. Validate by reducing timeout frequency on CI.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: Intermittent timeout occurs in the mochitest 'netwerk/test/browser/browser_necko_l10n.js' during the 'test_domain_change' subtest. Root cause is suspected race conditions and memory bloat in event dispatch code within dom/network/Connection.cpp and Connection.h. Failures manifest as OOM crashes and memory bloat logs primarily on linux1804-64 CI builds. Focus debugging on event dispatch functions and JS GC interactions to fix flaky test.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The analysis is thorough but too verbose and mixes speculative root causes with facts, reducing clarity. It lacks explicit references to specific functions or methods involved in the failure within 'browser_necko_l10n.js' and 'Connection*.cpp/h', which would help target the investigation. Reproduction steps could be more concise and self-contained. The proposed fix is vague, missing a clear stepwise plan. The optimized_context improves focus but remains wordy and lacks precise code locations. Overall, the analysis needs trimming and more precise artifact hints and implementation guidance to maximize AI effectiveness.
